meaning: 钂欓粯鎬濋儭[鑻卞浗濞佸皵澹師閮″悕]
examples: Both archaeologists quoted the 12th-century monk Geoffrey of Monmouth as saying the stones were thought to have medicinal properties.
Isn't this guy from Monmouth State?
Osbaldo is one of a lucky batch to be accepted to Monmouth College in western Illinois.
Monmouth Tory David Davies shook his head to make clear his opposition to the re-branding idea.
And in south Wales, properties in Maesteg, Bridgend, Ebbw Vale and Monmouth were also flooded.
